title: Contains Duplicate II
description:
Given an integer array `nums` and an integer `k`, return `true` if there are
two distinct indices `i` and `j` such that `nums[i] == nums[j]` and
`abs(i - j) <= k`.

solutions.summary: Remember each value’s most recent index and check the gap.

steps.title: Start from the function shape
steps.explanation: Begin with the signature and a default `false` return.

steps.title: Map each value to its last index
steps.explanation: `last[value]` holds the most recent position we saw that value.

steps.title: Walk the array with indices
steps.explanation: Process each value together with its index `i`.

steps.title: Check the gap to the last occurrence
steps.explanation: If we have seen the value within `k` indices, we found a nearby duplicate.

steps.title: Record the current index
steps.explanation: Otherwise update the value’s latest index and continue.

solutions.summary: Keep a set of the last k values; a hit inside it is a nearby duplicate.

steps.title: Start from the function shape
steps.explanation: Begin with the signature and a default `false` return.

steps.title: Hold a window of recent values
steps.explanation: The set will contain at most the last `k` values.

steps.title: Drop values that fell out of the window
steps.explanation: Once the index exceeds `k`, remove the value that is now too far back.

steps.title: Check then add the current value
steps.explanation: If the value is already in the window it is a nearby duplicate; otherwise add it.
